sanskrit:
महर्षीणां भृगुरहं गिरामस्म्येकमक्षरम्।
यज्ञानां जपयज्ञोऽस्मि स्थावराणां हिमालयः॥
transliteration:
maharṣīṇāṃ bhṛgur ahaṃ girām asmy ekam akṣaram |
yajñānāṃ japa-yajño ’smi sthāvarāṇāṃ himālayaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Among great sages I am Bhṛgu; among utterances I am the single syllable Oṃ; among sacrifices I am the japa-sacrifice; among immovables I am the Himalaya.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names four archetypal peaks—wisdom (Bhṛgu), sacred sound (Oṃ), effortless discipline (japa), and unshakeable presence (Himalaya)—showing Arjuna that the highest expressions of human potential are already facets of his own Self.
psychological_state: A moment of vertical alignment: the ego glimpses that its loftiest aspirations (wisdom, devotion, stability) are not outside achievements but inner recognitions, collapsing the gap between ideal and identity.
trigger_scenario: You’re about to speak at a major conference; stage fright surges. Remembering this verse, you silently chant Oṃ, feel the ‘Himalaya’ of your spine steady, and realize the authority is already within—not to be attained but to be allowed.
anchor_text: Realize the highest within you: wisdom, sacred sound, steady effort, and unshakable presence.
